name: Chili-Rubbed Chicken with Barbecue
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco)
2 3 1/2 pound chickens, quartered, with bones removed
3 cups mesquite wood chips, soaked in cold water for 1 hour (optional)
recipeInstructions:
For Chili Rub: Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
For Mop: Mix the first five ingredients in a medium bowl.
Arrange the chicken in a single layer on a large baking sheet. Season with salt and pepper. Sprinkle the chili rub generously on both sides of the chicken, pressing to adhere. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
Prepare the barbecue (medium-high heat). When the coals are white, drain the chips if using, and scatter over the coals. Place the chicken, skin side down, on the grill rack away from direct heat. Cover the grill and cook the chicken until cooked through, turning every 5 minutes and covering the grill, about 35 to 40 minutes (the chili rub may look slightly burned). Serve hot or warm, passing the mop separately.
6 Servings
Bon Appétit July 1995
